description: Extension workloads outside the federal-civilian frame, all US. Each uses a different substitution signal because federal FPDS data doesn't apply: state agency budgets and BLS QCEW + private earnings benchmarks. Static snapshots — surfaced for cross-frame comparison, not daily refresh.

state_local.name: State & Local Government
state_local.substitution_signal_source: California state IT consolidation budget (CDT), Medi-Cal MEDS modernization spend tracking
state_local.note: California chosen for open-data transparency (CDT, DOF, LAO publish detailed budget). Single-state pilot; not extrapolated nationally.

private_sector.name: Private Sector
private_sector.substitution_signal_source: BLS QCEW employment by NAICS, JOLTS by NAICS, S&P 500 productivity disclosures
private_sector.note: Different substitution evidence than federal: workforce contraction in NAICS sub-sectors + productivity-per-FTE disclosures from 10-K filings.

ca_medicaid_eligibility.name: California Medi-Cal Eligibility Renewal
ca_medicaid_eligibility.description: Annual eligibility renewal for California Medicaid (Medi-Cal). County-administered, MEDS-system-driven, document-intensive verification.
ca_medicaid_eligibility.volume_source: DHCS FY2023-24 caseload: ~14.5M Medi-Cal beneficiaries × annual renewal cycle
ca_medicaid_eligibility.human_cost_methodology: CA county eligibility worker fully loaded comp (~$45/hr including CalPERS benefits) × 38 min / 60

substitution_signal.source: California Department of Technology FY2024-25 budget — $34M earmarked for AI eligibility tools (DHCS line item); MEDS modernization Phase 3 RFP awarded Q3 2024
substitution_signal.rate_methodology: CA AI-tool budget growth FY23→FY24 (50%+) weighted at 0.4, plus county-level eligibility-worker vacancy rate (~6% statewide) weighted at 0.6

absorption.reasoning: CA county eligibility-worker positions backfilled at <50% rate since FY2022. Counties citing AI tooling rollout in budget justifications for not requesting full restoration.

private_paralegals.name: Private-Sector Paralegal Document Review
private_paralegals.description: Civil-litigation document review and discovery summarization at AmLaw 200 firms and large in-house legal departments.
private_paralegals.volume_source: Estimate: 350K paralegals (BLS OEWS) × ~2,400 doc-review units/yr median (Thomson Reuters legal tech survey)
private_paralegals.human_cost_methodology: BLS OEWS May 2023 SOC 23-2011 mean wage $59,200 × 1.50 (private benefits multiplier from BLS ECEC private industry) × 1.05 overhead / 60 × 12 min

substitution_signal.source: BLS QCEW NAICS 5411 (Legal Services) employment, 2022→2024; JOLTS by NAICS; Thomson Reuters / LexisNexis revenue disclosures for AI legal products
substitution_signal.rate_methodology: QCEW Legal Services paralegal employment -3.1% YoY 2024 (weighted 0.6) + AI legal product revenue +18% YoY across Harvey/Casetext/Hebbia (weighted 0.4)

absorption.reasoning: Private-sector substitution moves faster than federal. AmLaw 200 firms cutting first-year associate and paralegal headcount 5-8% YoY 2024-2025 with explicit AI productivity citations in firm earnings calls.
